paper.title_en: The impact of Hawking radiation from primordial black holes on recombination and the Hubble tension
paper.abstract_en: Primordial black holes (PBHs) evaporate through Hawking radiation, emitting high-energy photons and ionising their surrounding environment. As contributors to the density of dark matter, Omega_C, if 10^-18 solar mass PBHs are present in the Universe they delay recombination and move the surface of last scattering of the cosmic microwave background (CMB). We perform recombination simulations using the software Recfast, and calculate the PBH fraction of dark matter required to resolve the Hubble tension. We find that nominally a cosmic PBH energy density of Omega_PBH ~ 10^-3 Omega_C would cause an 8.9% increase in the value of H_0, enough to entirely reduce the tension between the early- and late-time observations. This PBH fraction is modified by Gray Body Factors affecting Hawking radiation. Furthermore, also fitting the CMB leaves the Hubble tension not fully relieved with our present PBH prescription. Until relevant non-gravitational properties of the dominant dark matter species are ruled out, we suggest that the hypothesis that the ionisation history of the universe matches the thermal history of the standard LCDM cosmology is too precarious to hang the expansion rate on, and that it is better to measure H_0 locally at z <~ 1.

decision.selection_reason_zh: 该论文探讨了原初黑洞的霍金辐射对再电离和哈勃常数张力的影响，提出了通过原初黑洞能量密度缓解哈勃张力的可能性，内容新颖且方法严谨。
decision.title_zh: 原初黑洞的霍金辐射对再电离和哈勃张力的影响
decision.abstract_zh: 原初黑洞通过霍金辐射蒸发，发射高能光子并电离其周围环境。作为暗物质密度Omega_C的贡献者，如果宇宙中存在10^-18太阳质量的原初黑洞，它们会延迟再电离并移动宇宙微波背景的最后散射面。我们使用软件Recfast进行再电离模拟，并计算解决哈勃张力所需的原初黑洞暗物质比例。我们发现，通常情况下，宇宙原初黑洞能量密度Omega_PBH ~ 10^-3 Omega_C会导致H_0值增加8.9%，足以完全缓解早期和晚期观测之间的张力。这个原初黑洞比例会受到影响霍金辐射的灰体因子的修正。此外，拟合CMB后，我们目前的原初黑洞方案并未完全缓解哈勃张力。在主导暗物质种类的相关非引力性质被排除之前，我们建议，宇宙的电离历史与标准LCDM宇宙学的热历史相匹配这一假设过于脆弱，不足以作为膨胀率的基础，最好在z <~ 1的局部测量H_0。
